PREFACE. 


All  the  Works  included  in  the  Bihliotiieca  Londinensis  will  be  found  in 
tlie  '•  Loudon  Catalogue  of  Books  published  in  Great  Britain  during  the 
Years  18il — 40,  arranged  in  One  Alphabet,  containing  their  Sizes,  Prices, 
and  Publishers'  Names." 

It  is  hardly  necessary,  in  a  preface,  to  say  much  of  a  work  of  this  kind,  as 
it  in  a  great  measure  speaks  for  itself.  The  want  of  some  work  that  would 
give  a  complete  list  of  the  hooks  'puhUshed  on  ptarticular  subjects,,  ivithout 
wading  through  a  general  catalogue,  has  been  long  complained  of;  and  at  the 
repeated  solicitations  of  those  who  were  daily  experiencing  the  inconvenience, 
the  present  volume  was  undertaken. 

The  labour  and  research  necessary  to  bring  this  book  through  the  press 
can  only  be  kuowai  to  those  who  are  accustomed  to  pursuits  connected  with 
bibliography.  Errors  it  is  wholly  impossible  to  avoid.  The  classification  of 
thirty-six  thousand  books,  ranging  over  a  period  of  thirty  years,  cannot  pos- 
sibly be  accomplished  without  immense  care.  However,  although  it  is  not 
expected  that  any  catalogue  can  be  complete,  it  may  be  permitted  here  to 
state  that  the  delay  in  publishing  the  Bibijotheca  Londinensis  has  been 
solely  occasioned  by  the  desire  to  make  it  as  perfect  as  possible  in  repeatedly 
examining  the  different  divisions  of  classification. 

This  book  contains  a  great  amount  of  information ;  and,  however  imper- 
fectly the  task  of  compiling  it  is  accomplished,  there  is  no  doubt  that,  to 
every  one  connected  with  literature,  it  will  save  much  valuable  time  in 
searching  after  all  the  works  written  by  different  authors  on  the  same 
subjects ;  whilst  to  those  who  buy  books,  it  shows  them  at  one  glance  the 
selection  they  can  make,  without  being  subject,  from  an  imperfect  memory, 
to  purchase  what  is  subsequently  found  useless. 

The  design  of  this  work  is  new,  and  was  suggested  in  consequence  of  many 
persons  referring  to  the  "  London  Catalogue"  being  unable  to  find  out  the 
books  required.     They  knew  perhaps  the  title  of  the  book,  but  not  the  name 


iv  I'REFACE. 

of  the  iuillior;  Imt  tliis  diHiculty  is  now  in  a  j^'ivat  measure  removed,  and  it 
is  presumed  iliai  the  liiiiLiuiHKcv  LoNDiNi'.xsis  will  be  found  at  least  a  valu- 
able assistant,  if  not  a  rade  viecuni,  to  those  whose  pleasure  or  business  it 
may  be  to  I'tmsult  its  [jaj^es. 

Ill  adiiitimi  to  the  Classiricatiou  of  Divisidns,  a  copious  table  of  reference  to 
the  leadiujf  subjects  will  be  found  at  the  end  :  and  facing  page  I  is  a  notice 
which  explains  the  plan  of  the  work. 

THOMAS   HODGSON.

NOTICE. 


The  words  in  Italic  indicate  the  first  word  of  the  line  in 
the  London  Catalogue  of  Books.  When  there  is  no  Italic 
in  the  line,  the  first  word  in  the  Bibliotheca  Londinensis 
is  the  same  as  in  the  London  Catalogue. 

The  cross  references  in  the  work,  when  printed  in  small 
CAPITALS,  point  to  a  different  division  of  classification  ;  but 
when  printed  in  the  usual  manner,  they  apply  to  another  part 
of  the  same  division  or  alphabet. 

The  General  Table  of  Reference  is  placed  at  the  end  of 
the  book.

TiiJ<: 
LONDON    CATALOGUE    OF    BOOKS, 

WITH    THEIR   SIZES,    PRICES,    AND    PUBLISHERS'    NAMES,    THOROUGHLY 
REVISED  AND  CORRECTED,  FROiM  1814  TO  1»10. 

"  '  The  London  Catalogue  of  Books,'  a  work  of  great  utility  to  Students,  and  to  all  admirers 
of  literature,  has  just  been  published  by  Mr.  Hodgson,  of  Paternoster  Row.  It  is  a  catalogue 
of  all  works  published  in  Great  Britain  from  1814  to  1846,  with  their  sizes,  prices,  and 
publishers'  names.  It  has  been  compiled  with  great  diligence,  and  at  an  immense  expense. 
Its  value  for  reference  will  at  once  be  understood,  when  we  say  that  it  contains  the  names  of 
nearly  40,000  publications,  so  classified  and  arranged  that  any  particular  work  can  be  instantly 
picked  out.  For  all  persons  at  all  engaged  in  literary  pursuits,  or  desirous  to  complete  or  make 
additions  to  a  library,  this  excellent  catalogue  will  prove  as  useful  as  '  The  London  Directory  ' 
is  found  to  the  merchant,"  &c. — Britannia. 


BENT'S    LITERARY    ADVERTISER, 

Htcjisttr  of  33oo!ts,  IBngrabfngs,  Src 

EXCLUSIVELY    DEVOTED    TO    LITERATURE    AND    THE    FINE    ARTS. 

Published  on  the  10th  day  of  every  month,  price  Id  stamped,  and  Qd  unstamped;  or  annual 
subscription,  8s,  including  the  Supplement,  or  Catalogue  of  all  the  New  Books  and  New 
Editions  published  during  the  year. 

Contents  : — Advertisements  of  New  Books,  New  Editions,  Books  in  the  Press,  Works  on 
the  Fine  Arts,  Miscellaneous  Advertisements  relating  to  all  branches  of  Literary  Property, 
&.C.,  &c.  A  correct  List  is  given  of  all  the  New  Books  and  New  Editions  that  are  published 
in  this  Kingdom,  with  their  Sizes  and  Prices.     And  a  List  of  the  New  French  Works. 

This  Periodical  has  now  been  established  forty-six  years,  and  was  the  first  organ  of  Pub- 
lishers in  announcing  their  productions  to  the  Trade,  the  Public,  &c.  In  producing  monthly  a 
list  of  those  books  that  are  published  from  month  to  month,  immense  care  and  research  are 
necessary  in  compiling  a  correct  one. 

The  London  Catalogue  of  Books  has  naturally  its  existence  from  this  source,  and  it  is  not 
necessary  to  enumerate  the  advantages  of  a  work  of  that  description — they  are  too  well  known. 

The  Editor,  therefore,  takes  this  opportunity  of  recommending  Authors,  Publishers,  &c., 
to  send  announcements  of  their  works  for  insertion  in  Bent's  Literary  Advertiser,  as  it 
insures  them  a  correct  description  in  the  London  Catalogue  of  Books. 


London  :  Published  by  THOMAS  HODGSON,  Aldine  Chambers,  Paternoster  Row, 
and,  by  order,  of  all  Booksellers  and  Newsmen.
